questions.prompt: Why is CAPI now mandatory for serious accounts?

choices.text: Pixel fires from the browser and is suppressed by iOS 14.5 ATT, Safari ITP, ad blockers, and privacy extensions — losing 20-40% of conversion events. CAPI sends server-to-server and carries clean hashed user data (email, phone, IDs) that boosts Event Match Quality. Combined with pixel via event_id deduplication, pixel + CAPI gives Meta the complete signal it needs to predict EAR accurately and match users for lookalike/retargeting. Enabling CAPI typically lifts attributed conversions 10-25%.

questions.explanation: CAPI recovers iOS/browser-blocked events. Critical foundation for ML signal.

choices.text: Deduplication failure. Pixel + CAPI both firing Purchase events without a shared event_id — Meta counts each separately, doubling reported conversions. Fix: generate a per-conversion event_id (the order ID from your e-commerce platform works well) and send the same ID in both pixel and CAPI fires; Meta will dedupe. Confirm in Events Manager that combined Purchase count ≈ actual order count, not 2x. Also check currency mismatches — sending USD value for EUR orders inflates reported ROAS.

questions.explanation: Missing shared event_id = double-count. Use order ID as event_id.

choices.text: After iOS 14.5 ATT, iOS attribution signal degrades quickly past 24 hours, and Meta needed a defensible default that survives the privacy environment. Click is the dominant signal — a verified action linking user to ad. View-through is real but inflates easily, especially for users who would have converted anyway. Read 7-click for daily decisions; check 28-click monthly if sales cycle is long; treat view-through skeptically for direct-response campaigns.

questions.explanation: Post-iOS 14.5, click is durable, view is fragile, 7 days is defensible.

questions.prompt: Lift test finds retargeting at 0.5x incremental ROAS, prospecting at 1.8x. Move?

choices.text: Reallocate toward prospecting. Retargeting's 0.5x means it's mostly claiming credit for sales that would have happened anyway (cart-abandoners coming back regardless). Prospecting is delivering genuine new revenue. Shift 20-40% of retargeting budget to prospecting; retest in 6-12 months. Don't kill retargeting fully — some lift remains and frequency caps still benefit from a retargeting layer — but rebalance to where the causal money is.

questions.explanation: Move budget toward incremental winners. Common finding: retargeting overcredited.

choices.text: 20-30% budget increments at most (40% for Advantage+); 3-4 days between raises; watch CPA at each step. If CPA holds within 10-20% of baseline, next raise is safe. If CPA jumps, hold or roll back — the campaign hit its current ceiling. Forcing more budget at the ceiling pays premium prices for marginal users. When vertical is capped, switch to horizontal: new geos, new angles, new offers.

questions.explanation: 20-30% increments, 3-4 day gaps, hold when CPA breaks.
